description: Add edit button + edit sheet to iOS item rows, change tap behavior (checkbox single-tap, row body double-tap), and add store color indicator in add-item bar

userStories.title: Edit Button on Item Row
userStories.description: As a user, I want a visible edit button on each item row so that I can quickly access item editing without long-pressing for the context menu
acceptanceCriteria: Every item row shows a pencil edit icon on the far right
The edit icon is visually subtle (secondary color) and does not compete with the item name or price
Tapping the edit icon sets state to present the edit sheet (US-002)
The edit icon appears on both checked and unchecked items
Xcode build succeeds
userStories.notes: Added @State editSheetItem and pencil button in itemRow trailing edge

userStories.title: Item Edit Sheet
userStories.description: As a user, I want a full edit sheet with all item fields (Name, Quantity, Price, Store, Category, Image) so that I can edit everything in one place without navigating context menu submenus
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the edit button opens a sheet with Name, Quantity, Price, Store, Category, and Image fields
All fields are pre-populated with the item's current values
Changing the store updates the category picker options
Cancel dismisses without saving
Save persists all changes and dismisses the sheet
Save is disabled when the name field is empty
Image section shows current thumbnail (if any) with change/remove options
Xcode build succeeds
userStories.notes: Created EditItemSheet.swift with Form-based editor. Wired in ListDetailView via .sheet(item: $editSheetItem). Save calls updateItem with change detection for clearStoreId/clearPrice.
userStories.testIntensityReason: New view with form logic, store-category dependency, and save flow

userStories.title: Checkbox Single-Tap Toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want only the checkbox circle to respond to single-tap toggling so that I don't accidentally check off items when tapping the row
acceptanceCriteria: Tapping the checkbox circle toggles the item (check/uncheck)
Tapping the item name, quantity badge, price, or empty space does NOT toggle the item
The checkbox has a minimum 44×44pt tap target
Context menu (long press) still works anywhere on the row
Swipe-to-delete still works on the row
Xcode build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Gesture refactor — must not break existing interactions

userStories.title: Double-Tap Row Toggle
userStories.description: As a user, I want to double-tap the row body to toggle check/uncheck so that I have an intentional way to toggle without using the checkbox
acceptanceCriteria: Double-tapping the item name, quantity badge, price area, or empty row space toggles check/uncheck
Single-tapping those same areas does nothing (no toggle)
Double-tap works on both unchecked items (to check) and checked items (to uncheck)
Single-tap on checkbox still toggles (not blocked by double-tap gesture)
Single-tap on edit button still opens edit sheet
Single-tap on image thumbnail still opens image picker
Long-press context menu still works
Swipe-to-delete still works
Xcode build succeeds
userStories.testIntensityReason: Gesture precedence is tricky — must coexist with single-tap, long-press, swipe

userStories.title: Store Color Indicator in Add-Item Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want the store picker to show a color-tinted storefront icon with the store name underneath when a store is selected so that I can immediately see which store is active
acceptanceCriteria: When a store is selected, the picker shows a storefront icon tinted in the store's color
Below the icon, the store name appears as a small caption, truncated to fit, in the store's color
If the store has no color, both icon and caption fall back to secondary color
When no store is selected, the storefront icon shows in secondary with no label (unchanged)
The label fits within the existing add-item toolbar without layout overflow
Xcode build succeeds
